description: A designed Department of Labor graphic crediting Trump for a strong economy via a stacked montage of real news-outlet headlines. The right half shows a full-length photograph of Donald Trump striding forward outdoors (greenery/White House lawn behind), wearing a dark navy suit, white shirt, long red tie, black belt and an American-flag lapel pin, with a serious expression. The left side, on a dark navy background, is headed with a large mixed serif title 'The Trump EFFECT!' ('The Trump' in italic, 'EFFECT!' in bold). Beneath are four torn-paper headline cards, each topped by a real outlet's logo banner in its brand color and a verbatim headline in black serif: POLITICO (red banner) 'Trump's economy bounces on consumer spending surge'; THE HILL (blue banner) 'New home sales surge in August to a 3-year high'; CNN Business (red banner) 'The US economy grew at a 3.8% rate in the second quarter'; Washington Examiner (black banner) 'Trump economy raises optimism to four-year high'. A small circular Department/agency seal sits centered at the bottom. The propaganda technique uses aggregated mainstream-media headlines as third-party validation to attribute positive economic news to Trump (the tweet: 'Thanks to @POTUS, our economy is HOT!'). Target audience: general public and pro-Trump supporters; intent is to claim economic credit using ostensibly neutral press citations.
notable_text: The Trump EFFECT! POLITICO: Trump's economy bounces on consumer spending surge. THE HILL: New home sales surge in August to a 3-year high. CNN Business: The US economy grew at a 3.8% rate in the second quarter. Washington Examiner: Trump economy raises optimism to four-year high.
